cards.text: Then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains.
cards.connection: Provides a biblical parallel for the strategic retreat of Ethiopian Christianity from the coastal plains to the highland strongholds.
cards.cueContext: Shown when discussing the twilight of Axum.

cards.text: And the woman fled into the wilderness, where she has a place prepared for her by God, in which she is to be nourished...
cards.connection: A profound parallel showing the Church (the woman) fleeing to the 'wilderness' (the highlands) for preservation.
cards.cueContext: Shown when discussing the monasteries as arks of preservation.

cards.text: Arise, shine, for your light has come, and the glory of the LORD has risen upon you... And nations shall come to your light.
cards.connection: King Lalibela's vision for a New Jerusalem in rock was a physical embodiment of this prophetic vision of a restored Zion.
cards.cueContext: Shown when discussing the construction of Lalibela.

cards.text: But you have come to Mount Zion and to the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem...
cards.connection: Demonstrates that the sacred is not confined to geographic Jerusalem; Lalibela embodied this spiritual truth.
cards.cueContext: Shown when reflecting on the meaning of the New Jerusalem.

cards.text: Now when the queen of Sheba heard of the fame of Solomon concerning the name of the LORD... 'Blessed be the LORD your God...'
cards.connection: The biblical foundation for the Queen of Sheba narrative, which the Kebra Nagast later expanded upon.
cards.cueContext: Shown when analyzing the Kebra Nagast and Solomonic legitimacy.

cards.text: Not by might, nor by power, but by my Spirit, says the LORD of hosts.
cards.connection: Highlights the tension between Amda Seyon's temporal military defense and the true spiritual strength of the monasteries.
cards.cueContext: Shown when discussing Amda Seyon and the monastic renewals.

cards.text: That they may all be one, just as you, Father, are in me, and I in you...
cards.connection: The legend of Prester John and contact with Europe reflected the underlying unity of the global body of Christ across centuries of isolation.
cards.cueContext: Shown when discussing contact with the wider Christian world.
